intro: Pasania maingayi, also known as the Pasania tree, is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae, native to Southeast Asia. The Pasania tree has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pasania maingayi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, the Pasania tree is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional remedies such as syrups and tinctures.
